The company reaffirmed FY 2026 guidance of at least £235m revenue and £35m profit, backed by new funding facilities and ongoing £25m share buyback programme.
retail_take: Strong operational momentum and guidance confidence should support positive sentiment; however, management flagged normalisation in Q2 demand and broader economic caution heading into H2, likely tempering enthusiasm among growth-focused investors.

headline: Board Changes
brief: Maintel Chair Stephen Beynon to step down; William Ginn joins as Independent Non-Executive Director.
summary: Stephen Beynon, Non-Executive Chair, intends to retire following completion of refinancing and equity fundraising, remaining until a successor is identified. Angus McCaffery and Clare Bates step down on 24 July 2026. William Ginn, 59, joins as Independent Non-Executive Director on 24 July, bringing finance expertise from the recent refinancing process. Board will announce additional appointments including new Chair in due course.
retail_take: Leadership transition at opportune moment after achieving refinancing milestone; incoming director has relevant transaction experience, suggesting continuity. Non-event to mildly positive.

headline: Long-Term Incentive Plan and Grant of Awards
brief: Empire Metals adopts LTIP, awards 20.25m shares/options to directors and staff with demanding vesting conditions.
summary: Empire Metals Limited established a Long-Term Incentive Plan granting 8.25m nil-cost shares from its Employee Benefit Trust and 12m options at 40p exercise price to directors, senior management and employees. Managing Director Shaun Bunn and Finance Director Greg Kuenzel each received 6m awards (3m shares, 3m options); Non-Executive Phil Brumit received 750,000 (250,000 shares, 500,000 options). Total awards represent 2.67% of issued capital. Vesting requires share price targets of 50p-98p over four tranches, each requiring 10 consecutive trading days at target. Chairman Neil O'Brien and Non-Executive Peter Damouni received no awards. Outstanding scheme commitments now total 12.70% of capital.
retail_take: Standard LTIP adoption with materially demanding vesting conditions (98p target represents +145% premium), aligning management incentives with shareholder value creation. Non-dilutive EBT structure and excluded independent directors suggests reasonable governance. Routine disclosure with positive governance signal.

headline: Holding(s) in Company
brief: BlackRock crosses 6.18% threshold in Caledonia Mining, up from 6.22% previously held.
summary: BlackRock, Inc. notified Caledonia Mining Corporation PLC on July 15, 2026 that it crossed a notification threshold on July 14, 2026. BlackRock's total voting rights position stands at 6.18% (5.00% direct shares, 1.18% via financial instruments comprising 0.78% securities lending and 0.39% CFDs), down slightly from the previous position of 6.22%. The position represents 1,196,365 voting rights.
retail_take: Routine disclosure of a marginal position change by a major institutional investor; minimal market significance given the slight decrease and BlackRock's typical passive index-tracking role.

headline: Issue of Share Options
brief: CelLBxHealth issues up to 50m share options to employees at 1.35p exercise price, including 11.4m to Chief of Staff Michelle Munson.
summary: CelLBxHealth's Nominations/Remuneration Committee approved issuing up to 50m share options to employees, with 11.4m allocated to PDMR Michelle Munson (Chief of Staff). Exercise price set at 1.35p, the closing mid-market price on 15 July 2026. Options vest over three years with time-based tranches (one-third after year one, then quarterly) plus performance condition requiring share price to close at or above 2.5p on 10 out of 20 consecutive trading days. Total outstanding options will reach approximately 55.8m, representing 4.9% of issued and to-be-issued share capital.
retail_take: Standard employee retention mechanism aligned with strategic execution; vesting conditions tie rewards to both tenure and share price recovery (85% uplift from grant price), signalling management confidence in turnaround efforts, but dilutive to existing shareholders.

headline: Preliminary Results
brief: Northern Bear reports FY26 revenue growth to £86.1m with improved margins; proposes 7.5p total dividend.
summary: Northern Bear Plc delivered revenue of £86.1m (up 10.2% YoY) with gross profit of £22.0m at 25.5% margin. Operating profit reached £5.1m and basic EPS 30.2p. The group repaid £1.5m debt and achieved net cash of £6.2m. A final dividend of 2.5p plus special dividend of 5.0p per share proposed for September 2026. H1 performance was strong but H2 weakened due to weather and macroeconomic headwinds. Management expects FY27 revenues broadly in line with FY26, supported by strong forward order book.
retail_take: Strong operational delivery with improved profitability and balance sheet, offset by H2 softness and cautious FY27 guidance amid sector challenges; market likely reads as solid but tempering growth expectations.

headline: Board change
brief: Johnson Matthey appoints Joachim Rosenberg as independent Non-Executive Director effective 16 July 2026.
summary: Joachim Rosenberg has joined Johnson Matthey's board as an independent Non-Executive Director, effective from the conclusion of the 16 July 2026 AGM. He will serve on the Audit, Nomination and Remuneration Committees. Rosenberg brings 20 years of Volvo Group executive experience across Europe and Asia, plus McKinsey consulting background, with expertise in automotive, strategic M&A, and international operations. He holds current NED positions including Concentric AB (delisted 2024) and previous tier-1 automotive supplier and aluminium industry board roles.
retail_take: Relevant automotive and strategy expertise strengthens board amid industry transition; appointment of experienced international operator likely viewed positively given Johnson Matthey's transformation needs.

headline: Directorate change
brief: SSE appoints John Pettigrew as independent non-Executive Director from 1 December 2026.
summary: John Pettigrew, former Chief Executive of National Grid plc (10-year tenure), joins SSE's Board as independent non-Executive Director on 1 December 2026. He brings 30+ years of multinational energy leadership experience and will serve on the Nomination Committee, Safety/Sustainability/Health/Environment Advisory Committee, and Energy Markets Risk Committee. His appointment aligns with SSE's £33bn energy transition investment plan through 2030.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: high-calibre, sector-relevant appointment strengthens governance for major capex programme; validates SSE's strategic direction with proven regulated energy expertise.

headline: Helical lets the 12th floor at The Bower
brief: Helical lets 12th floor of The Bower to Audio Network; occupancy rises to 96.6%.
summary: Helical PLC has agreed an 11-year lease (with six-year break option) for 9,572 sq ft on the 12th floor of The Bower, EC1, to Audio Network Limited, a global music licensing company. The lease is priced in line with March 2026 ERV. Audio Network will relocate in November following minor alterations. This letting increases The Bower's occupancy to 96.6%, demonstrating strong leasing momentum at the Old Street campus.
retail_take: Positive signal: near-full occupancy (96.6%) at flagship asset validates asset quality and London tech district appeal; demonstrates operational execution but lacks financial impact detail.

headline: Manufacturing agreement with Plowshare Therapies
brief: Oxford Biomedica wins manufacturing deal with Plowshare Therapies for AAV gene therapy programme at Durham, NC facility.
summary: Oxford Biomedica has signed an agreement to provide process development and AAV GMP manufacturing services to Plowshare Therapies, a biotech company developing gene therapies for rare genetic diseases. The work will be supported from OXB's FDA-approved, commercial-scale viral vector manufacturing facility in Durham, North Carolina, acquired in October 2025. The partnership reflects growing demand for OXB's AAV capabilities and demonstrates client diversification across its expanded US manufacturing network.
retail_take: Positive signal: demonstrates momentum in OXB's AAV business, validates Durham acquisition, and shows ability to attract new clients—supporting growth narrative in viral vector CDMO space.

headline: Together successfully prices second RMBS of 2026
Investor Presentation
brief: Brave Bison publishes investor presentation for potential offer to acquire remaining System1 Group shares.
summary: Brave Bison Group PLC (AIM: BBSN) has published an investor presentation regarding a possible offer for System1 Group plc shares not already owned. Brave Bison currently holds a 28% stake in System1, a UK-based marketing research platform listed on AIM. The presentation follows an announcement on 10 July 2026 and is available on Brave Bison's investor website. The company operates across eight countries with ~350 employees through three divisions: Consultancy & Marketing Services, Sport & Entertainment, and Marketing Skills & Capabilities (MiniMBA platform).
retail_take: Market-neutral disclosure of pre-bid investor materials; substance depends on offer terms, financing, and System1 shareholder response, yet to be determined.

headline: Swedish nuclear legislation enacted
brief: Swedish nuclear legislation enacted, removing uranium mining regulation as nuclear facility, eliminating municipal consent requirement.
summary: Sweden's Parliament voted 15 June to amend the Nuclear Activities Act, enacted 15 July as planned. Uranium mining will no longer be regulated as a nuclear facility, removing the need for explicit municipal consent for mine development. This follows the January 2026 removal of the uranium exploration and mining ban and April 2026 designation of Aura's Häggån deposit as a deposit of national interest by Sweden's Geological Survey.
retail_take: Regulatory progress removes development friction for Aura's Swedish uranium project; positive milestone but project remains early-stage with historical resource estimates still being converted to current standards.

headline: Notice of Interim Results
brief: Evoke PLC to announce interim results for H1 2026 on 12 August at 7.00am BST.
summary: Evoke PLC, the betting and gaming company operating brands William Hill, 888, and Mr Green, will release its interim results for the six months ended 30 June 2026 on Wednesday 12 August 2026 at 7.00am BST. The Gibraltar-incorporated, London-listed group is among the world's leading gaming operators. Contact details for investor enquiries are provided via CEO Per Widerström, CFO Sean Wilkins, and Investor Relations Director James Finney.
retail_take: Routine results announcement notification; market will await the actual earnings data on 12 August to assess operational performance and guidance.

headline: Director/PDMR Dealing
brief: CFO Ash Mehta purchases 60,000 shares at average 96.5p, increasing stake to 0.48%.
summary: Celebrus Technologies CFO Ash Mehta bought 60,000 ordinary shares on 14-15 July 2026 at an average price of 96.5 pence per share (50,000 at 96.0p and 10,000 at 99.0p). His total beneficial holding is now 180,070 shares, representing approximately 0.48% of voting rights. The purchase demonstrates insider confidence in the AIM-listed data solutions provider.
retail_take: Insider buying by senior management signals confidence in company prospects, though modest stake increase suggests limited conviction or available capital for larger purchases.

headline: Appointment of Chief Financial Officer
brief: Inspecs Group appoints Robert Brodie as CFO, joining in autumn 2026. Outgoing CFO Chris Kay to support transition.
summary: Robert Brodie, aged 51, a Chartered Management Accountant with 20+ years' finance leadership experience, will join Inspecs as Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director in autumn 2026, succeeding Chris Kay who remains as consultant. Brodie previously served as CFO at City Energy Network and Steripack Group, and earlier at Direct Healthcare Group and Crown Cork & Seal, bringing expertise in M&A, business integration and international operations. Several entities where Brodie was director were placed into liquidation or administration in early 2026 due to cessation of the UK Government's ECO4 scheme.
retail_take: Seasoned CFO appointment with strong manufacturing and operational background supports growth strategy; liquidations were scheme-driven rather than performance-related, reducing concern. Positive signal for investor confidence.

headline: Notice of Results and Investor Presentation
brief: SDI Group to announce FY 2026 results on 29 July with investor webcast presentation.
summary: SDI Group plc, an AIM-listed buy-and-build group specialising in specialist lab equipment, industrial sensors and scientific products, will release audited final results for the year ended 30 April 2026 on Wednesday, 29 July 2026. A webcast for retail investors will be held the same day at 2.00 p.m. BST via the Investor Meet Company platform, allowing shareholders to submit pre-event questions and participate live. The company operates a portfolio of businesses across aerospace, defence, manufacturing, semiconductors, life sciences and healthcare sectors.
retail_take: Routine trading announcement scheduling results and investor engagement event; no material substance disclosed; market will await actual results for investment implications.

headline: Dotdigital Loyalty and new AI capabilities
brief: Dotdigital launches Loyalty product and AI capabilities to expand customer lifecycle platform and revenue opportunities.
summary: Dotdigital Group (AIM: DOTD) has launched Dotdigital Loyalty, a native loyalty programme tool for Shopify merchants, alongside Agents in Dotdigital—a natural language interface for core platform workflows—and an MCP server connecting the platform to customer LLMs like ChatGPT and Claude. These launches extend the company's connected customer lifecycle proposition across acquisition, retention, personalisation and advocacy, creating cross-sell opportunities within its existing customer base and expanding addressable marketing spend.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: product launches strengthen platform differentiation, deepen AI integration, and broaden revenue per customer without requiring acquisition spend, validating recent strategic acquisitions.

headline: Investor Presentation via Investor Meet Company
brief: Savannah Resources to present Phase 1 Definitive Feasibility Study for Barroso Lithium Project via investor webinar on 20 July.
summary: CEO Emanuel Proença and CFO Henrique Freire will present the Phase 1 Definitive Feasibility Study for the Barroso Lithium Project on 20 July 2026 at 10:00 BST via Investor Meet Company platform. The project, designated a Strategic Project under the EU Critical Raw Materials Act, is Europe's largest spodumene lithium deposit and received €110m Portuguese State grant approval in January 2026. The facility will produce 183,000 tonnes per annum of spodumene concentrate, supporting approximately 500,000 vehicle battery packs annually.
retail_take: Presentation of completed feasibility study is likely viewed positively as a major milestone de-risking the project, though actual market impact depends on study results and development timeline details disclosed.

headline: Publication of Circular and Notice of GM
brief: Supermarket Income REIT publishes circular for shareholder vote on planned share issue.
summary: Supermarket Income REIT (LSE: SUPR) has published its shareholder circular detailing a proposed share issue, with a General Meeting scheduled for 3 August 2026 at Macfarlanes LLP in London. The share issue is conditional on UK Admission approval and shareholder approval via Resolution at the GM. Board members including Nick Hewson, Sapna Shah, and others representing ~0.22% of existing shares have committed to voting in favour. The circular is available on the company website and being posted to shareholders.
retail_take: Routine capital-raising disclosure; procedural announcement of shareholder meeting on proposed equity issuance with management support already secured; market likely to await voting outcome.

headline: Notice of Half Year 2026 Financial Results
brief: Seplat Energy to release H1 2026 results on 30 July with webcast presentation at 12:00pm BST/WAT.
summary: Nigeria's leading indigenous energy company Seplat Energy will announce its Half Year 2026 Financial Results on Thursday 30 July 2026. Executive Management will host a webcast presentation at 12:00pm BST/WAT, with the recording subsequently available on the company's website. Seplat operates 11 PMLs, 17 PPLs and 5 OMLs across onshore and shallow water locations in the Niger Delta, plus interests in three export terminals and multiple gas processing plants, positioning it as a key supplier to Nigeria's domestic power generation market.
retail_take: Routine regulatory notice announcing earnings release date and webcast; no material information disclosed. Market impact depends on results substance when published 30 July.

headline: Earlham & Orwell gas development concept
brief: Orcadian Energy launches Earlham Gigagrid, proposing offshore gas-to-power data centre complex with carbon capture to monetise high-CO2 gas fields.
summary: Orcadian Energy (AIM: ORCA) commenced the Assessment Phase for developing its 100%-owned Earlham and Orwell gas fields (licence P2680) with a novel concept: a 200MW offshore power station with integrated carbon dioxide capture, supplying a co-located data centre. Earlham contains 114 bcf of methane (49% CO2 content), with Orwell adding 31 bcf. The company established Earlham Gigagrid Ltd to incubate the project and assigned P2680 to a Gigagrid subsidiary. Orcadian secured a deferred repayment schedule for £1.34m owed to Independent Power Corporation, now due by 31 December 2027 at 8.5% interest. The concept targets hyperscalers and neo-scalers (Google, Amazon, Meta, CoreWeave, etc.) as ultimate developers, with potential for gigawatt-scale expansion without grid stress or government subsidy.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively as transformative revaluation of undermonetised gas asset and potential equity-dilution-free capital raise through Gigagrid, though regulatory approvals and third-party investment remain key execution risks.

headline: Trading and Operations Update July 2026
brief: Pharos Energy reports strong H1 2026 production of 5,650 boepd, on-track drilling programmes, and plans additional appraisal well.
summary: Pharos delivered 5,650 boepd net production in H1 2026 (Vietnam 4,583 boepd, Egypt 1,067 bopd), within FY guidance. Five of six Vietnam wells completed on time and budget; Egypt resumed drilling with first well completed 9 July. H1 revenue was $82m. Cash position strengthened to $45.2m (vs $40.2m end-2025); Egyptian receivables reduced to $1.7m from $7.4m. Company proposes drilling additional TGT-20X appraisal well for $4m. FY 2026 capex remains ~$50m. Final dividend 0.9317p per share approved; under Ratio Petroleum takeover offer, shareholders entitled to 28p per share total consideration.
retail_take: Solid operational delivery and improving cash position support the recommended Ratio takeover at 28p per share; drilling momentum and strong premiums demonstrate asset quality but market will focus on bid certainty.

headline: Trading Update
brief: Shoe Zone upgrades FY2026 profit guidance after strong May-June trading and warehouse clearance sale.
summary: Shoe Zone achieved above-market sales in May and June 2026, supported by a warehouse closing-down sale and favourable half-term weather. The company has improved its cash position accordingly. The Board now expects an adjusted loss before tax of no greater than £1.0m for the year ending 3 October 2026, a material improvement from prior guidance of £1.0m-£2.0m issued on 22 April 2026.
retail_take: Positive read: upgraded guidance and improved cash position suggest operational momentum and better cost control, likely supporting share price near-term despite underlying loss-making position.

headline: Multiple Contract Wins Worth c. US$4m
brief: MTI Wireless Edge secures ~US$4m in multiple contracts across all three divisions with H2 2026 deliveries.
summary: MTI Wireless Edge (AIM: MWE) announced approximately US$4m in new orders spread across its three divisions: MTI Summit (US$0.6m power amplifier solutions to Israeli defence); PSK (US$2.4m for military data room solutions and antenna work); commercial antennas (US$0.5m for RFID and 5G backhaul); and Mottech (US$0.6m irrigation solution to Arabian Gulf customer). Majority delivery expected by end-Q1 2027, with most arriving in H2 2026, supporting FY26-27 order book growth.
retail_take: Positive signal showing diversified demand recovery across all business units and improved conversion of bids to orders, with near-term revenue visibility supporting growth outlook.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Plus500 repurchased 63,616 shares at 4,172-4,246p as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: Plus500 purchased 63,616 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 through Panmure Liberum at prices ranging from 4,172p to 4,246p, with volume-weighted average of 4,200.82p. Shares will be held in treasury. Post-purchase, 69,112,798 shares remain in issue (excluding treasury), with company holding 45,775,579 treasury shares. Total voting rights now 69,112,798. Buyback commenced 16 February 2026 under programme announced 9 February 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation; ongoing buyback signals management confidence but represents non-event for markets absent broader strategic context or share price impact.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Glanbia purchases 17,885 shares at €23.43 average as part of €50m buyback programme through September 2026.
summary: Glanbia plc bought 17,885 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 via J&E Davy on Euronext Dublin at a volume-weighted average price of €23.4257 per share, with trades ranging from €23.30 to €23.52. These shares will be cancelled. The purchase forms part of the company's €50m share buyback programme announced 25 February 2026, running until 30 September 2026. Post-settlement, 238,935,967 ordinary shares remain in issue.
retail_take: Routine capital allocation activity; buyback continuation signals management confidence in valuation but represents modest progress against €50m programme target with four months remaining.

headline: Fourth quarter and full year trading update
brief: Dunelm reports FY26 sales growth of 3.1% to £1,825m with PBT in line with guidance; digital now 42% of sales.
summary: Dunelm delivered FY26 total sales of £1,825m, up 3.1% year-on-year, with Q4 sales at £428m (+2.9%). Gross margin improved 10bps to 52.5%, while PBT meets previous guidance and consensus expectations of £210m. Digital participation grew to 42% of total sales (up 2 percentage points), supported by app launch and AI shopping assistant beta trial. Strong cash conversion of c.70% of operating profit enabled £141m in dividend payments despite £7m insurance income from store fire closures. The group opened a new Kingston-upon-Thames superstore and plans upper end of five-to-ten new store guidance for FY27.
retail_take: Solid execution on guidance with modest growth, margin expansion, and strong cash generation should be broadly reassuring, though low single-digit sales growth in competitive market may not excite; strategic progress signals confidence.

headline: Half Year Results
brief: Ocado H1 results show strong international momentum and improved profitability, though core business revenues decline ex-closures.
summary: Ocado reported H1 FY26 revenue of £1,037m (up 54% including £354m non-recurring closure fees from Kroger/Sobeys network optimisation). Excluding closure impacts, revenue grew 1% to £684m. Adjusted EBITDA jumped to £432m (up 371%) including £351m from closures; underlying EBITDA was £81m (down 12% YoY). Technology Solutions saw 27% international volume growth with new Asda partnership signed. Ocado Retail achieved 15% revenue growth and returned to profitability (£12m adjusted EBT vs £17m loss prior year). Group maintains £1.1bn liquidity. Guidance: FY26 cash flow positive in H2, full-year positive in FY27, with £150m cost reduction programme underway.
retail_take: Market likely sees mixed signals: positive on international growth trajectory, Asda win, and Retail profitability turnaround, but negative on core Technology Solutions margin compression (23% ex-closures vs 26%) and ongoing cash burn before H2 inflection.

headline: Extension of PUSU Deadline
brief: ECP and KKR extend takeover deadline for DCC to 27 July; improve offer with conditional Nexora proceeds adjustment.
summary: The Consortium (Energy Capital Partners and KKR) has improved its takeover proposal for DCC plc. Shareholders would receive 6,525p base cash consideration plus up to 125p additional cash tied to DCC Technology (Nexora) sale proceeds exceeding $800m, plus the 147.22p final dividend. The PUSU deadline has been extended to 5pm London time on 27 July 2026 to finalise Nexora documentation and other transaction terms. Confirmatory due diligence is complete and definitive documentation substantially agreed.
retail_take: Improved bid with meaningful upside contingency signals serious intent but introduces execution risk on Nexora proceeds; market likely views positively as evidence of deal progress, though contingent element creates uncertainty on final value.

headline: Trading Update
brief: Somero reports better-than-expected H1 2026 trading; upgrades FY 2026 revenue guidance ahead of market expectations.
summary: Somero Enterprises improved trading in H1 2026 driven by US non-residential construction stabilization, its largest market. Demand for large-line Boom Screeds rebounded after three-year decline, aided by new S-22EZ+ and S-15EZ models. New Hammerhead Ride-on Screed attracting customers. North America expected to show significant improvement versus subdued H1 2025. Europe and Australia also improved. Board now expects FY 2026 revenue ahead of market expectations (prior $86.0m), with commensurate profit and cash generation improvement. Full results due 8 September 2026.
retail_take: Positive read: Somero returns to growth trajectory after cyclical downturn, with innovation driving demand recovery and guidance raised, though management notes early-stage stabilization with ongoing macro uncertainty.

headline: Half-year Financial Report
brief: Crest Nicholson reports H1 losses amid weak housing market; takes cost-cutting action while negotiating covenant amendments.
summary: Crest Nicholson posted an adjusted operating loss of £11.9m (HY25: £11.9m profit) on revenue of £197.6m (down 20.8%) for the six months to April 2026, with home completions falling 21% to 584 units. Gross margin collapsed to 7.0% from 14.2%, driven by lower volumes, reduced land profits, and £3.6m in NRV provisions on apartment developments. The company is negotiating covenant amendments with lenders, securing temporary waivers until 30 September 2026, whilst executing Project Elevate restructuring. FY26 guidance projects 1,400-1,500 completions, £40m land sales revenue, and net debt of £100m-£120m.
retail_take: Market likely to view negatively: margin collapse, loss swing, and covenant discussions signal distress despite operational improvements and lender support. Recovery timing remains uncertain given softening demand post-April.

headline: Final Results
brief: Frasers Group reports FY26 results showing revenue growth of 8.7% to £5.3bn, but adjusted profit fell 4% amid macro headwinds and impairments.
summary: Revenue rose 8.7% to £5,325.9m, driven by 59.2% international growth. Adjusted profit before tax declined 4% to £538m due to £259.5m asset impairments and £37.5m higher interest costs, offset partly by £33.8m Coventry Arena disposal gain and £117.7m strategic investment gains. Retail gross margin improved 150bps to 47.1%. Frasers Plus reached 1.1m active customers and 20.5% of UK online sales. The group launched takeover offers for HUGO BOSS (€38 per share) and Accent Group (AUD $0.65 per share) post year-end, with net assets rising 23.4% to £2,452.7m.
retail_take: Mixed signals: strong top-line growth and margin expansion offset by profit decline, heavy impairments, and macro uncertainty delaying guidance. Strategic acquisitions show ambition but add execution risk.

headline: Final Results
brief: MS International reports flat revenues of £115m but profit down 25% to £15.06m; strategic focus on Defence division amid board transition.
summary: FY2026 revenues £115.01m (2025: £117.50m) with profit before tax down to £15.06m from £20.05m; EPS 67.4p (2025: 90.0p). Cash strengthened to £46.53m. Defence & Security division delivered sales ahead of budget; Forgings faced US tariff headwinds; merged Petrol Station/Branding division shows progress. Chairman Michael Bell steps down after 50 years; Anthony Wreford becomes independent Non-Executive Chairman; Michael O'Connell appointed Group CEO. Company pursuing sale of Petrol Station Superstructures & Branding division with encouraging buyer interest; final dividend 20p per share.
retail_take: Mixed signals: profit decline and delayed defence orders are concerning, but strong cash position, strategic restructuring, and experienced leadership transition could support long-term growth. Market likely cautious pending M&A completion and defence order conversion.

headline: Q3 AuM update
brief: Premier Miton's AuM fell to £8.6bn in Q3 from £10.3bn year-ago, with £837m net outflows but improving performance and stable gross sales.
summary: Premier Miton Group reported AuM of £8.6 billion at 30 June 2026, down from £10.3 billion a year earlier. Q3 net outflows totalled £837 million, concentrated in previously underperforming strategies including UK equity (-£153m) and international equity (-£436m). The group closed two funds (Diverse Income Trust and Premier Miton Global Sustainable Optimum Income Fund) accounting for £217 million of outflows. However, gross sales remain resilient at approximately £3 billion annualised, investment performance has improved across several key strategies including European equities, and new US equity management arrangements have delivered 700 basis points of outperformance since implementation.
retail_take: Mixed picture: significant AuM decline and concentrated outflows are concerning, but improving performance, stable sales pipeline, and management's targeted actions suggest flow stabilisation is underway. Market likely sees cautious recovery narrative rather than terminal decline.

headline: Recommended cash acquisition of Gooch & Housego
Results of Annual General Meeting
brief: Wynnstay Properties PLC reports all AGM resolutions passed by poll vote on 15 July 2026.
summary: Wynnstay Properties PLC held its Annual General Meeting on 15 July 2026, where shareholders approved all ordinary and special resolutions by poll vote. Full resolution details are available on the company website. No specific voting percentages, shareholder numbers, or material business outcomes were disclosed in the announcement.
retail_take: Routine governance disclosure with all resolutions passing; no indication of shareholder dissent or controversial matters; non-event for market pricing.

headline: AGM Statement
brief: RS Group reports solid FY2026/27 start with continued momentum across all regions ahead of AGM.
summary: RS Group plc provided an AGM update on 16 July 2026 noting a solid start to FY2026/27 with positive market momentum from Q4 FY2025/26 continuing into Q1, combined with strong execution of its multi-year improvement plan delivering good progress across regions. Analyst consensus for year ended 31 March 2027 forecasts revenue of £3,014 million, adjusted operating profit of £282 million and adjusted PBT of £261 million, compared to prior year revenue of £2,881 million.
retail_take: Pre-AGM trading update signals business momentum and execution confidence; modest revenue growth implied (~4.6%) with margin expansion suggests positive operational trajectory likely to be well-received.

headline: Advantage NRG secures £3.4m of contracted works
brief: Advantage NRG secures £3.4m in new National Grid contracts, bringing FY2026 total to £20.8m.
summary: Hercules' subsidiary Advantage NRG has won two contracts worth £3.4m combined on the National Grid electricity transmission network. The East of England contract (£2.6m) runs until June 2027 with 18 peak operatives, while the North Wales contract (£0.8m) runs July-October 2026 with 13 operatives. This brings Advantage NRG's total contracted work in FY2026 to £20.8m, demonstrating strong market demand for overhead transmission line services.
retail_take: Positive signal: strong order book momentum for recently acquired specialist subsidiary validates acquisition strategy and suggests robust underlying UK infrastructure demand.

headline: Updated MRE Incorporates Heavy Rare Earth Elements
brief: NeoTerra's Monte Muambe fluorspar project updated resource estimate now includes heavy rare earth elements, potentially boosting revenues by 40%.
summary: NeoTerra Group (LSE: TERA) updated its Mineral Resource Estimate for the Monte Muambe Project in Mozambique to incorporate heavy rare earth elements for the first time. The company estimates potential annual production of 650 tonnes of HREE-rich concentrate alongside 50,000 tonnes of acid-grade fluorspar, with the by-product potentially increasing projected revenues by up to 40% over mine life. The HREE recovery requires only modest incremental capital and operating costs, as it would use the existing fluorspar flotation circuit after primary processing.
retail_take: Material upside to project economics with minimal additional capex required; validates Monte Muambe as multi-commodity asset. Market likely views positively as it strengthens near-term monetisation case with high-demand critical minerals.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Ruffer Investment Company Limited repurchased 250,000 Redeemable Participating Preference Shares at GBP 2.929173 per share on 15 July 2026, representing 0.065% of issued shares. The shares are held in treasury. Following this transaction, 384,017,764 shares remain in issue, with 90,604,714 held in treasury and 293,413,050 voting rights outstanding.
retail_take: Routine share buyback executed under existing shareholder authority; modest scale suggests portfolio management rather than material capital activity. Standard disclosure with no material implications.

The buyback was conducted under a programme announced on 30 September 2025.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity; modest share count reduction supports EPS but signals no material strategic development or valuation concerns.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: M.P. Evans Group PLC bought back 2,419 of its own shares for cancellation on 15 July 2026 through broker Cavendish Capital Markets. Shares traded between 1,608p and 1,644p, with a volume-weighted average price of 1,630.86p. Following cancellation, the company now has 52,164,649 shares in issue, representing total voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback reducing share count; modest scale suggests ongoing capital management rather than market signal, likely neutral reception.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: TRIG buys back 270,000 shares at 73.36p per share as part of ongoing programme.

retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under existing programme; modest scale transaction signals continued capital return but limited market catalyst. Non-event unless sustained buyback activity accelerates.

headline: Q2 2026 Trading Update
brief: Capital Limited reports Q2 2026 record revenue of $117.3m, up 34% YoY, but exits underperforming contracts.
summary: Capital Limited achieved Q2 2026 revenue of $117.3m (34.2% YoY growth) with H1 2026 at $219.0m (37.6% YoY). Mining revenue surged 264.3% to $25.5m; MSALABS grew 36.8% to $23.8m; drilling revenue up 7.9% to $68.0m. The company demobilised from Nevada Gold Mines and Sadiola amid portfolio optimisation, with one-off costs offsetting strong operational performance. Full-year 2026 guidance of $410-$440m maintained. Investment portfolio valued at $116.5m (up from $97.5m at year-end 2025) generated 7.6% H1 returns.
retail_take: Strong organic growth and diversification offset by strategic exits and termination costs; market likely sees disciplined portfolio management but near-term earnings headwinds from demobilisation expenses.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: INPP purchased 150,000 shares at 139.9p per share on 15 July, to be held in treasury.
summary: International Public Partnerships Limited bought 150,000 ordinary shares at 139.9 pence per share through Deutsche Numis Securities on 15 July 2026. The shares will be held in treasury. Following the transaction, the company holds 121,027,505 treasury shares with 1,790,215,627 shares in issue (excluding treasury). The infrastructure investment company currently trades at 139.8p with a 6.29% dividend yield.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

retail_take: Routine share buyback for cancellation reducing share count; modest capital return to shareholders; no material market-moving implication given scale.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

summary: Mercia Asset Management PLC repurchased 250,000 ordinary shares between 9-15 July 2026 at a consistent price of 26.50p per share under its April 2026 buyback programme. Following the purchases, 421,879,557 shares remain in issue (excluding 11.6m treasury shares). The company intends to cancel the repurchased shares. Mercia manages £2.2bn in assets under management across venture, development capital, property finance and proprietary capital divisions.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution at steady pricing suggests management confidence in valuation and capital allocation; non-event for markets as programme was pre-announced.

This reduces total shares in issue from approximately 395.6m to 395.2m shares, with no shares held in treasury.
retail_take: Share buyback at modest valuations; accretive to EPS and suggests management confidence, but routine capital management activity likely read as non-event by markets.

headline: New Contract Award
brief: GMS secures four-year charter for self-elevating accommodation vessel in Brazil, deploying between August-November 2026.
summary: Gulf Marine Services PLC has signed a four-year charter agreement for one self-elevating accommodation unit with an international offshore accommodation services provider for deployment offshore Brazil. The contract comprises 1,461 days of firm charter and continues GMS's Latin America expansion strategy announced in May 2026. The vessel is scheduled for delivery to Rio de Janeiro between August and November 2026.
retail_take: Positive signal: long-term revenue secured for a new vessel, validates Latin America strategy, and demonstrates strong market demand for GMS's assets in new regions.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares

Since 1 July, the company has repurchased 840,389 shares for £8.49m. Treasury holdings now total 13.42m shares, with 492.18m shares in issue. The company intends to hold purchased shares in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management signalling confidence in valuation; buyback reduces share count and can support EPS, though modest scale suggests measured approach. Non-event for sentiment.

retail_take: Routine share buyback execution as part of ongoing capital management programme; modest scale suggests continuation of disciplined shareholder returns policy rather than significant strategic signal.

retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within disclosed programme parameters. Buybacks typically signal management confidence but market impact depends on capital allocation efficiency and whether share count reduction offsets earnings dilution. Non-event absent broader strategic context.

summary: On 15 July 2026, Greencoat Renewables PLC purchased 191,789 of its own ordinary shares on Euronext Dublin via RBC Europe Limited at a volume-weighted average price of €0.7588 per share (range: €0.7560-€0.7620). This transaction forms part of the buyback programme announced 5 March 2026.
retail_take: Routine capital management activity demonstrating continued shareholder return commitment; buyback at modest valuations likely viewed positively but represents incremental rather than transformative shareholder value creation.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: McBride PLC repurchases 3,243 shares at 160p each for cancellation under existing buyback programme.
summary: McBride plc purchased 3,243 ordinary shares on 13 July 2026 at 160 pence per share via Investec Bank plc on the London Stock Exchange, with all trades executed at the same price. The shares will be cancelled, reducing ordinary shares in issue to 173,815,106. This forms part of the company's existing buyback programme announced on 1 May 2026.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution under pre-announced programme; modest share count reduction supports earnings per share accretion. Non-event for most investors.

headline: Trading Statement - 18% cc bookings growth
brief: Trustpilot reports H1 2026 bookings up 18% at constant currency, driven by Enterprise and North America growth.
summary: Trustpilot expects H1 2026 bookings of $171m (up 18% cc YoY) with strong Enterprise momentum, ARR from high-value customers (+$20k) up 36% YoY. North America bookings grew 27% cc while revenue increased 19% cc. Gross retention improved to 87%, though net dollar retention declined to 101% from 103% following plan migrations. The company spent $42.5m on buybacks and remains on track for full-year high-teens revenue growth guidance. AI-driven commerce positioning strengthened with ChatGPT requests up 400% YoY and Trustpilot ranked #1 most-cited review platform globally by Seer Interactive research.
retail_take: Solid execution with beat-to-guidance bookings growth driven by AI tailwinds and North America traction; maintained profitability guidance suggests operational discipline despite cash deployment. Positive read likely but cash burn and retention normalisation warrant monitoring.

headline: Quarter 1 Trading Update
brief: Premier Foods Q1 shows strong branded revenue growth of 4.0%, on track for FY26/27 profit guidance.
summary: Premier Foods delivered Q1 branded revenue growth of 4.0% to £225.1m, with total revenue up 2.7% to £246.4m. UK branded revenue grew 3.8% led by Sweet Treats (6.6% growth), while Mr Kipling sales rose 9% driven by new product launches. Acquired brands—The Spice Tailor, FUEL10K, and Merchant Gourmet—all achieved double-digit growth and remain on track for £100m combined revenue. International revenue increased 6% with strong performance in North America and Europe. The company confirmed it remains on track to deliver FY26/27 trading profit expectations of £209.4m-£213.7m.
retail_take: Solid early-year execution with broad-based growth and market share gains supports maintaining consensus forecasts, likely viewed as reassuring rather than transformational by markets.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Grafton Group purchased 60,000 shares at £9.08 average price on 15 July as part of £25m buyback programme.
summary: Grafton Group PLC bought 60,000 ordinary shares for cancellation on 15 July 2026 via Goodbody Stockbrokers at a volume-weighted average price of £9.0846, with a range of £8.9560 to £9.1760 per share. This is part of its £25m share buyback programme announced 30 June 2026. To date, 719,174 shares have been repurchased across Goodbody and Deutsche Bank since the programme commenced.
retail_take: Share buyback signals management confidence in valuation and returns excess capital to shareholders; buyback activity typically supports share price but is largely priced-in for established programmes.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: IHG repurchased 1,000 shares at $156.60-$160.10 on 15 July; plans cancellation.
summary: InterContinental Hotels Group purchased 1,000 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 via Goldman Sachs International on the London Stock Exchange at prices ranging from $156.60 to $160.10, with a volume-weighted average price of $158.56. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, IHG has 148,612,282 ordinary shares in issue, excluding 5,431,782 held in treasury. The purchase was executed under authority granted at the May 2025 AGM.
retail_take: Routine capital management reflecting prior shareholder authorization; modest share count reduction supports EPS but signals no major strategic shift or undervaluation view.

headline: Completion of the acquisition of Knox Resources
brief: Alien Metals completes £200k acquisition of Knox Resources, securing 100% of Georgina Basin copper-gold project in Australia.
summary: Alien Metals (AIM: UFO) has completed its acquisition of Knox Resources from Venari Minerals for £200,000, comprising £100,000 cash and 90.26m shares at 0.11079p. Knox owns the Georgina Basin IOCG project in Northern Territory's East Tennant province covering 2,500km² with seven granted licences and three pending. Independent valuation by SRK estimates the project worth A$1.5–3.8m (preferred A$2.7m), suggesting acquisition at discount. Board changes now effective: Vincent Fayad appointed CEO, Michael Carter becomes Non-Executive Chairman. Project includes three drill-ready targets and A$4.8m prior exploration.
retail_take: Disciplined acquisition at apparent discount valuation diversifies Alien into copper-gold; market likely views positively given technical upside and established exploration framework, though significant share dilution (90m new shares) warrants scrutiny.

headline: Share Buyback
brief: EKF Diagnostics completes buyback of 100,000 shares at 25.5p per share; shares to be cancelled.
summary: EKF Diagnostics Holdings plc purchased 100,000 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 25.5 pence per share, totalling approximately £25,500. The shares will be cancelled, reducing total issued share capital from 430,123,112 to 430,023,112 ordinary shares. This represents a modest capital reduction for the AIM-listed global diagnostics business, which operates point-of-care analysers and life sciences operations across US and German manufacturing sites.
retail_take: Routine capital management action; buyback at consistent 25.5p price suggests orderly execution but relatively immaterial scale (0.023% share reduction) likely signals limited market impact either way.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: CT Healthcare Trust repurchased 10,000 shares at 137.60p per share on 15 July 2026 for treasury.
summary: CT Healthcare Trust PLC purchased 10,000 ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange on 15 July 2026 at an average price of 137.60p per share through J.P. Morgan Securities plc. The company intends to hold these shares in treasury. Following this transaction, the company holds 268.5m treasury shares and has 46.7m shares in issue.
retail_take: Routine share buyback for treasury; modest purchase size suggests measured capital allocation; neutral signal without broader context on buyback programme objectives.

headline: Trading Update
brief: Foxtons faces H1 headwinds from sales market downturn and lettings disruption; expects £8.5m H1 operating profit versus £12.3m prior year.
summary: Foxtons reported adverse trading in H1 2026 due to prolonged sales market weakness and Renters' Rights Act volatility, reversing £3m lettings revenue in May-June. Financial Services grew, but sales market deteriorated amid political uncertainty and high interest rates. The group delivered £4.5m annualised cost savings in H1. H1 2026 adjusted operating profit expected at £8.5m (H1 2025: £12.3m), with full-year 2026 guidance of £17m-£19m, heavily weighted to H2 as tenancy terminations normalise.
retail_take: Negative read likely. Significant profit decline versus strong prior-year comparator, meaningful revenue reversals from legislative change, and cautious 2026 outlook amid structural market headwinds signal weakness persists.

headline: Trading Statement
brief: DFS Furniture delivers £45m FY26 profit, beating downturn; debt falls sharply despite H2 market softening.
summary: DFS Furniture reported full-year PBTu(A) of around £45m, within upgraded guidance of £43-50m and up £15m year-on-year despite H2 market weakness driven by declining consumer confidence and housing transactions. Revenue grew 2.7% with gross margin expansion and cost discipline. Net bank debt fell to £69m from £107m, reducing leverage to 0.9x from 1.4x. Order intake declined 4.4% in H2 but full-year was -1.0%. Customer NPS improved 7% and colleague engagement rose 19% year-on-year.
retail_take: Market likely views this positively: DFS beat expectations amid challenging conditions, significantly deleveraged its balance sheet, and maintained market leadership. Resilience through downturn and improved financial flexibility supports medium-term ambitions of £1.4bn revenue and 8% PBT margin.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Caledonia Investments repurchased 37,709 shares at 381p each for cancellation.
summary: Caledonia Investments PLC purchased 37,709 of its own ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 at 381.0 pence per share through Peel Hunt LLP, totalling approximately £143.8m. The shares will be cancelled. Following this transaction, the company's issued share capital comprises 512,420,989 ordinary shares with voting rights.
retail_take: Share buybacks typically signal management confidence in valuation and return excess capital; modest positive read but routine capital management activity with no immediate market catalyst.

headline: Result of Conditional Placing and Subscription
brief: Clean Power Hydrogen raises £7.3m through placing and subscription at 1.5p per share, pivoting to capital-light licensing model.
summary: CPH2 successfully completed a fundraising raising approximately £7.3m gross (£2.54m firm placing, £0.61m conditional placing, £3.65m subscription, £0.5m retail offer) at 1.5 pence per share. This adds 318.3m new ordinary shares, resulting in 989.6m shares outstanding post-admission. Net proceeds fund transition towards capital-light model focused on strategic partnerships and global technology licensing. Conditional shares require shareholder approval at July 20 general meeting, with admission expected July 22.
retail_take: Positive signal on investor confidence in revised strategy, but significant dilution (49.27% of enlarged capital) and shift away from manufacturing may concern those backing original technology commercialisation plans.

headline: Q1 Trading Statement
brief: SSE reiterates 2026/27 EPS guidance of 168-193p; Q1 networks investment up 83% YoY to £0.9bn.
summary: SSE delivered 83% YoY increase in networks investment to £0.9bn in Q1, with £0.9bn deployed across regulated transmission and distribution businesses. Renewables output surged 31% YoY to 3,264 GWh, driven by favourable weather and capacity additions. The company maintains its £33bn five-year investment plan on track for £5bn annual spend, with major projects advancing including Netherton Hub, Dogger Bank B (30 turbines installed), Platin power station (180MW OCGT, operational 2028), and Western Isles HVDC link. EPS guidance reaffirmed at 168-193p for 2026/27 and 225-250p for 2029/30. Funding diversified with £2.4bn raised in debt at blended 4.8% cost.
retail_take: Positive read: robust capital deployment acceleration, strong renewables generation, maintained earnings guidance, and de-risked funding raise signal confident execution of long-term growth strategy, though guidance reiteration (rather than upgrade) may disappoint momentum traders.

headline: Trading update, first quarter
brief: Experian reports Q1 FY27 revenue growth of 10% at actual rates, 7% organic, in line with guidance. Full-year outlook unchanged.
summary: Experian delivered Q1 FY27 revenue growth of 10% (actual exchange rates) and 8% (constant currency), with 7% organic growth meeting expectations. North America (67% of group revenue) achieved 7% organic growth, with B2B up 11% driven by Financial Services and strong Ascend platform adoption. Latin America (15% revenue) posted 12% organic growth, bolstered by Brazil expansion and the closed idwall acquisition. Consumer Services declined 2% due to wind-down of data breach contracts; excluding those, growth was 3%. Full-year expectations remain unchanged. CEO Brian Cassin cited execution on data assets, scaled platforms, and AI opportunities.
retail_take: Market likely to view positively: ahead-of-expectations organic growth across key regions, strong B2B momentum, and strategic acquisitions (idwall) supporting future growth without guidance cuts.

headline: Quarterly Activities Report
brief: BHP reports record iron ore and ~2Mt copper production for FY26 with strong cost control and advancing growth pipeline.
summary: BHP delivered record iron ore production of 265Mt (up 1%) and maintained copper output at ~2Mt for the second consecutive year, with average realised copper prices up 35% to US$5.74/lb. All assets met unit cost guidance despite inflationary pressures. The company progressed copper growth projects including Cerro Colorado restart application in Chile, Copper South Australia expansion plans, and Argentina's RIGI approval for Vicuña. FY27 guidance: copper 1,650-1,800kt, iron ore 260-272Mt. Jansen potash project in Canada begins production in FY27, with ~US$2.3bn impairment recorded.
retail_take: Strong operational performance and record production with favourable commodity pricing support near-term earnings, though FY27 copper guidance declines 15-18% due to grade decline at Escondida, indicating longer-term growth dependency on development projects.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gaming Realms buys back 127,492 shares at 29.75-30.00p as part of ongoing programme.
summary: Gaming Realms PLC purchased 127,492 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 through Peel Hunt at prices ranging from 29.75p to 30.00p, with a volume-weighted average of 29.95p. The shares will be held in treasury. The company now holds 24.1m treasury shares with 272.2m shares in issue, representing total voting rights.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution; shareholders may view modest capital allocation positively if balance sheet supports it, though impact likely minimal given scale relative to market cap.

headline: CAA publishes update on Heathrow regulatory models
Issue of Equity
brief: Onward Opportunities issues 75,000 shares at 126.75p per share in response to investor demand.
summary: On 15 July 2026, Onward Opportunities Limited issued 75,000 new ordinary shares at 126.75 pence per share, priced at a premium to estimated net asset value. The issuance was in response to additional investor demand. Total ordinary shares in issue now stand at 34,116,594, with voting rights equal to share count.
retail_take: Capital raise at NAV premium suggests continued investor interest and strong portfolio performance; modest positive signal but routine for investment vehicles.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Gamma Communications repurchased 16,545 shares on 15 July at 917-945p as part of its ongoing buyback programme.

This brings total buyback programme purchases to 2,700,826 shares since January 2026. Post-settlement, Gamma has 91,063,525 shares in issue and 89,549,503 voting shares (excluding 1,514,022 treasury shares). The company remains in an offer period as announced 7 April 2026.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution during takeover offer period. Small daily tranche suggests disciplined, market-compliant programme management; no material capital allocation signal given ongoing offer context.

headline: Quarterly Update to 30 June 2026
brief: Metals Exploration reports Q2 2026 results with $25.2m free cash flow, $47.1m gold revenue; La India construction 56% complete, first gold on track for December 2026.
summary: Q2 2026 generated US$25.2m pre-tax free cash flow on US$47.1m gold revenue from 10,552oz sold at US$4,463/oz. All-in-sustaining-cost improved to US$2,019/oz. La India development in Nicaragua is 56% complete with US$123.9m spent of US$177m budget; first gold pour remains December 2026. Runruno mine in Philippines maintained production with 84.4% gold recovery. Company securing US$27m mining equipment loan from Nicaraguan bank (first drawdown August 2026). Exploration expanded with Batong Buhay porphyry copper-gold project agreements signed in Philippines and La India South drilling showing wide epithermal zones.
retail_take: Solid operational quarter with maintained cash generation from core Runruno mine funding La India development on schedule; modest decline in production and lower gold prices offset by improved costs, but quarter-on-quarter revenue down ~11%. Market likely views positively given on-track development timeline and exploration upside, though declining cash balance and lower production warrant monitoring.

headline: Q2 2026 OPERATING UPDATE
brief: Thor Explorations reports Q2 production of 19,153oz gold and maintains FY2026 guidance of 75,000-85,000oz.
summary: Thor Explorations produced 19,153 ounces of gold in Q2 2026, generating $77.3m in revenue at an average realised price of $4,535/oz. The Segilola mine processed 240,769 tonnes at 2.57g/t grade with 93.3% recovery. The company maintains FY2026 production guidance of 75,000-85,000oz and All-in Sustaining Cost guidance of $1,000-$1,200/oz. Q2 cash balance reached $207.5m with adjusted net cash of $225.6m. Over 20,000 metres of drilling occurred across exploration portfolio, with advanced discussions underway with Senegal's government on the Douta Project Mining Convention expected Q3 2026.
retail_take: Solid mid-year performance with unchanged guidance and strong cash position signals operational stability; Douta Project advancement and exploration results pipeline may support future growth, though FID timing remains uncertain.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Edinburgh Investment Trust purchased 85,000 shares at 814.93p per share on 15 July 2026.
summary: Edinburgh Investment Trust plc repurchased 85,000 ordinary shares (25p each) on the LSE through Investec Bank plc at an average price of 814.93p on 15 July 2026. The shares will be held in treasury. Following this transaction, the company holds 69,649,709 treasury shares out of 195,666,734 issued shares, leaving 126,017,025 shares with voting rights in circulation.
retail_take: Routine share buyback; typical capital management action for investment trusts to manage discount/premium and support earnings per share, unlikely to materially move the market.

headline: Trading Update
brief: Ramsdens upgrades FY26 profit guidance to £32-35m from £30-33m on strong trading across all divisions.
summary: Ramsdens Holdings has raised its FY26 profit before tax forecast to £32-35m (from £30-33m) despite gold prices falling over 20% from January peaks. Pawnbroking lending reached record levels with June marking a record month; the loan book grew £1m to £15.5m in six weeks, supporting FY27 earnings. The precious metals segment benefits from inventory accumulated at higher gold prices. Jewellery retail remains resilient, and foreign currency card loads are 40% ahead year-on-year. The company continues its store expansion programme with Hereford and Skegness opened, and Newark and Peterborough in development.
retail_take: Positive read—strong trading momentum across diversified segments offsets gold price volatility, with management demonstrating confidence despite macro uncertainty. Upgrade reflects operational execution rather than external tailwinds, supporting the pending FirstCash acquisition narrative.

headline: Purchase of Own Shares
brief: AdvancedAdvT completes further share buyback, purchasing 10,000 shares at 158.01p.
summary: AdvancedAdvT Limited purchased 10,000 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 at 158.01 pence per share, held in treasury. Cumulative buyback programme total now reaches 5,228,100 shares since March 2026 announcement. Following this purchase, total voting rights stand at 131,897,706 shares out of 137,125,806 issued shares.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution under previously announced programme; signals capital deployment confidence but represents non-event absent broader strategic context.

headline: Q3 Trading Update
brief: Diploma upgrades FY26 guidance for second time; Q3 organic growth of 15% drives 7% profit upgrade.
summary: Diploma PLC reported 15% organic revenue growth in Q3, with strong performance across Controls, IS Group, Clarendon, Peerless and Windy City Wire. The group upgraded FY26 organic revenue guidance to 14% (from 12%), with operating margins now expected at c.26.5% (from c.25%), representing c.42% operating profit growth—a further 7% upgrade to consensus. The CDM acquisition completed 25 June, establishing a US interconnect defence platform. Strong acquisition pipeline and balance sheet headroom noted.
retail_take: Second consecutive upgrade signals robust trading momentum and execution confidence; 42% profit growth significantly outpacing organic revenue suggests substantial margin expansion and acquisition accretion, likely driving positive market re-rating.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: AEP Plantations repurchased 57,803 shares at 165-169p as part of its July buyback programme.
summary: AEP Plantations PLC purchased 57,803 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 at prices ranging from 165p to 169p, with a volume-weighted average of 167.65p, through Cavendish Capital Markets. This brings total shares repurchased under the buyback programme announced 6 July to 362,560 shares held in treasury. Post-purchase, the company has 399,762,720 shares in issue with 383,230,920 voting rights outstanding.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution within announced parameters; supports EPS accretion but no strategic surprise. Markets likely indifferent absent material capital allocation context.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: GlobalData repurchased 94,429 shares at 73.55p average on 15 July as part of ongoing buyback programme.
summary: GlobalData purchased 94,429 ordinary shares on 15 July 2026 through Investec Bank at prices ranging from 73.00p to 74.70p, with a weighted average of 73.55p. The shares will be cancelled. Following completion, 745,903,598 shares remain in issue with no treasury holdings. Total voting rights are therefore 745,903,598.
retail_take: Routine buyback execution within previously announced programme; modest daily repurchase volume at relatively tight price range suggests orderly execution with no operational concerns.

headline: Discount management clarification
brief: VEIL clarifies discount management methodology, recalculating NAV premium/discount figures using GBP-quoted shares instead of illiquid USD class.
summary: Vietnam Enterprise Investments Ltd issued a clarification on 16 July 2026 regarding its June 2026 buyback announcement. The Investment Manager recalculated average premium/discount to NAV figures using the actively-traded GBP-denominated ordinary shares (VEIL.LN) rather than the illiquid USD share class (VEID). June buyback data: 47,392 shares purchased for $487,788 at a 12.27% discount to NAV. All share quantities and values remain unchanged; only the NAV calculation methodology was corrected.
retail_take: Administrative correction with no material financial impact; the company proactively identified and fixed a technical reporting issue. Demonstrates transparency but suggests prior methodology was flawed.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: Baltic Classifieds repurchased 2.29m shares at 174-183p/share during 9-15 July for cancellation.
summary: Baltic Classifieds Group PLC purchased 2,287,544 ordinary shares between 9-15 July 2026 through Merrill Lynch International at weighted average prices ranging from 174.10p to 182.48p per share for cancellation. Daily volumes ranged from 282,993 to 580,041 shares. Following completion, issued share capital stands at 425,964,739 shares with no treasury holdings. Total consideration approximately £4.1m based on weighted average prices across the five trading days.
retail_take: Share buyback signals management confidence in valuation and capital allocation; reduces share count, supporting EPS accretion. Routine but modestly positive signal absent other market headwinds.

headline: Annual Financial Report of the Guarantor
brief: Northumbrian Water Finance PLC publishes guarantor's annual financial report for year ended 31 March 2026.
summary: Northumbrian Water Limited has filed its Annual Report and Financial Statements for the financial year ended 31 March 2026 as guarantor of bonds issued by Northumbrian Water Finance PLC. The filing, submitted to the FCA's National Storage Mechanism on 15 July 2026, is now available for public access. The document provides audited financial information for the water utility operator serving Northumbrian Water and Essex & Suffolk Water regions.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of guarantor financials for bond documentation purposes; no material news or earnings surprises evident; unlikely to move markets materially.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: HICL Infrastructure repurchased 350,000 shares at 134.41p on 15 July; now holds 166.4m treasury shares.
summary: HICL Infrastructure PLC purchased 350,000 ordinary shares through Investec Bank plc on 15 July 2026 at a weighted average price of 134.407p, with a range of 134.00p to 134.80p. The company intends to hold these shares as treasury stock. Following this transaction, HICL holds 166,445,988 treasury shares, with 1,865,042,073 shares in active issue. Total voting rights excluding treasury shares stands at 1,865,042,073.
retail_take: Share buyback at modest scale signals capital management and potential earnings per share accretion; routine treasury operation with no material strategic implication for shareholders.

headline: Statement regarding the proposed issue of a prospectus
brief: Three Albion VCTs plan to raise up to £40m in prospectus top-up offers for 2026/27 tax year.
summary: Albion Crown VCT, Albion Enterprise VCT, and Albion Technology & General VCT plan aggregate fundraising of £40m with optional over-allotment facilities of up to £20m. Crown VCT targets £10m, while Enterprise and Technology & General each target £15m with £10m over-allotment options. Prospectus expected August 2026; applications open September 2026. This enables VCT investors to subscribe for tax year 2026/27.
retail_take: Routine fundraising announcement for VCT vehicles; provides investment opportunity for qualifying UK investors seeking tax-efficient exposure to venture capital portfolio.

headline: Transaction in Own Shares
brief: PSH repurchased 37,033 shares on 15 July 2026 at average price of $51.93, reducing share count.
summary: Pershing Square Holdings purchased 37,033 public shares on 15 July 2026 at an average price of $51.93 USD across two listings: 25,566 shares on PSH (LSE) at 3,856 pence average and 11,467 shares on PSHD (LSE) at $51.81 average. NAV per share was $77.06 as of 14 July. Post-buyback, PSH has 173.9 million public shares outstanding with 37.1 million shares held in treasury.
retail_take: Routine share buyback execution at discount to NAV supports shareholder returns; typical capital management activity with no material implications for valuation or strategy.

headline: Statement regarding the proposed issue of a prospectus
brief: Albion VCTs announce £40m prospectus top-up offer for 2026/2027 tax year, with additional £20m over-allotment facilities.
summary: Three Albion VCTs (Crown, Enterprise, Technology & General) plan to raise up to £40m aggregate through share subscription offers in the 2026/2027 tax year. Albion Enterprise VCT and Technology & General VCT each target £15m with £10m over-allotment facilities; Albion Crown VCT seeks £10m with no over-allotment. Prospectus expected August 2026; applications open September 2026. Individual board approval required for over-allotment facility usage.
retail_take: Routine VCT capital raise announcement ahead of new tax year; positive signal of ongoing investment activity in venture capital vehicles but not material for existing shareholders until prospectus details emerge.

headline: NBPE Announces Transaction in Own Shares
brief: NBPE repurchased 30,000 Class A shares on 15 July 2026 at £14.70-£15.00, reducing outstanding shares to 40.8m.
summary: Neuberger Private Equity Partners Limited executed a share buyback of 30,000 Class A shares on 15 July 2026 at prices between £14.70 and £15.00 per share, utilising general authority granted by shareholders on 11 June 2026. All repurchased shares will be cancelled, reducing outstanding Class A shares from 40,859,149 to 40,829,149. The company retains 3.15m shares in treasury.
retail_take: Routine capital management action signalling modest shareholder returns; buyback at modest discount suggests confidence in valuation, though immaterial scale suggests no major read-through for market direction.

headline: Appointment of Non-Executive Director
brief: Foresight Enterprise VCT appoints James Barnes as non-executive director effective 1 August 2026, replacing retiring Michael Gray.
summary: James Barnes, with extensive experience in financial services, investment banking, and retail management including a successful Dobbies Garden Centres tenure and CEO role until 2013, joins the board as non-executive director. He currently serves as director of Blackrock Smaller Companies Investment Trust and previously chaired Dunedin Smaller Companies Investment Trust. Barnes will join the Audit, Management Engagement, Remuneration, and Nomination Committees. The appointment follows Michael Gray's retirement, with Kavita Patel succeeding as Chair after nearly three years on the board.
retail_take: Routine board succession announcement with experienced appointee; management continuity maintained through internal promotion to chair. No material implications for investors.

headline: Q2 and H1 2026 Production Report
brief: Kenmare's H1 2026 production missed expectations due to WCP A commissioning delays, though shipments tracking guidance.
summary: Kenmare Resources reported H1 2026 production below forecast, with ilmenite output of 273,100 tonnes down 39% YoY and HMC production down 34% YoY to 442,200 tonnes, primarily due to slower-than-expected commissioning of upgraded Wet Concentrator Plant A. However, total shipments of 555,600 tonnes tracked annual guidance run-rate, supported by finished product stockpile drawdowns. Net debt increased to $175.7m from $158.8m, but the company secured a $30m credit facility uplift, and zircon markets strengthened with strong demand for new ZrTi concentrate product.
retail_take: Mixed read: production misses offset by on-track shipments and improved zircon pricing, but rising debt and commissioning delays signal operational challenges ahead despite near-term visibility.

headline: Statement regarding the proposed issue of a prospectus
brief: Albion VCTs announce prospectus top-up offer targeting £40m aggregate fundraising in 2026/27 tax year.
summary: Three Albion VCTs (Crown, Enterprise, Technology & General) plan to raise up to £40 million in aggregate, with additional over-allotment facilities of up to £20 million combined. Crown VCT targets £10 million; Enterprise and Technology & General each target £15 million with £10 million over-allotment options. Prospectus expected August 2026; share applications open September 2026. AlbionVC manages portfolio including Quantexa (unicorn, £2.6bn valuation), Oviva, Elliptic, and other deeptech/fintech/healthtech companies.
retail_take: Routine VCT capital raise announcement; positive signal of manager confidence in portfolio opportunities, though typical for VCT cycle. Market likely neutral—procedural disclosure with standard regulatory pathway.

headline: Nalunaq mine underground drilling results
brief: Nalunaq gold mine delivers high-grade underground drilling results confirming near-term production confidence and exploration upside.
summary: Amaroq reported exceptional underground drilling results from Nalunaq Gold Mine in Greenland, with 37 drill holes averaging 42.8 g/t Au. Sixteen holes exceeded 30 g/t Au (the MRE4 average resource grade), and 30% exceeded 60 g/t Au, including peak intersections of 132.5 g/t Au over 0.5m and 132.0 g/t Au over 0.5m. The company maintains a rolling 12–24 month production inventory and is shifting to a planned 810 Exploration Drive targeting untested western areas of the Main Vein, with potential to unlock ~2Moz exploration target. Results fall outside the forthcoming MRE5 data cut-off, providing additional upside.
retail_take: Market likely reads positively: high-grade ore confirmation de-risks production ramp-up, supports operational flexibility, and hints at material resource expansion ahead of LSE uplisting, though results are post-resource-estimate and carry standard exploration optionality.

company: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust Plc
headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust discloses voting rights denominator at 90.4m shares.
summary: As at 16 July 2026, BlackRock Greater Europe Investment Trust plc had 90,447,866 ordinary shares of £0.001 each in issued capital, excluding 27,481,072 treasury shares. This figure serves as the denominator for FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules calculations regarding shareholder notification thresholds under DTR 5.6.1. The announcement is a routine regulatory disclosure of voting rights and capital structure.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure providing the share count denominator for threshold calculations. No material implications for investors or trading activity.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Frontiers Investment Trust updates voting rights denominator to 164.97m shares as of 16 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock Frontiers Investment Trust plc disclosed that as at 16 July 2026, its issued share capital comprised 164,966,520 ordinary shares, each carrying one vote per share, with 76,856,281 shares held in treasury. The company notified the market of this figure in accordance with FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1, establishing the denominator shareholders must use to calculate disclosure thresholds for material shareholding notifications.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure updating the voting rights denominator. No material implications for investors; purely administrative notification required under FCA rules.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock American Income Trust discloses 61.16m voting shares outstanding as at 16 July 2026.
summary: BlackRock American Income Trust plc notified the market of its voting rights structure as at 16 July 2026, reporting 61,160,138 ordinary shares in issue, each carrying one vote per share. The company holds 34,201,167 shares in treasury. The 61,160,138 figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting share capital and treasury holdings. No substantive implications; standard transparency requirement with no read-through for investors.

headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust updates voting rights denominator to 244.8m shares.
summary: As of 16 July 2026, BlackRock Smaller Companies Trust plc has 244,828,950 ordinary shares of £0.25 each in issued capital, excluding 53,863,655 treasury shares. This figure serves as the denominator for shareholders calculating whether they must notify interests under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules (DTR 5.6.1). The disclosure is made in accordance with Article 15 of the Transparency Directive.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure establishing the share count for notification threshold calculations; no material implications for investors or trading activity.

headline: Dividend Declaration
brief: Artemis UK Future Leaders declares first interim dividend of 3.85p per share, flat year-on-year.
summary: Artemis UK Future Leaders plc has declared a first interim dividend of 3.85p per share for the year ending 31 January 2027, unchanged from the prior year comparator of 3.85p. The dividend will be paid on 21 August 2026 to shareholders on the register as of 24 July 2026, with ex-dividend date of 23 July 2026. The company targets a 4% dividend yield based on year-end share price, aiming to distribute all portfolio income and enhance payments through realised capital gains.
retail_take: Stable dividend maintenance reflects consistent income generation and adherence to stated policy; the flat year-on-year payout suggests no deterioration in underlying portfolio performance but also no growth acceleration.

company: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust Plc
headline: Total Voting Rights
brief: BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust updates voting rights denominator to 100.2m shares.
summary: As at 16 July 2026, BlackRock Energy and Resources Income Trust plc had issued capital of 100,197,197 ordinary shares of £0.01 each, excluding 35,388,997 shares held in treasury. The company notified the market that shareholders must use 100,197,197 as the denominator for calculating disclosure thresholds under FCA Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules provision 5.6.1, determining when shareholding notifications are required.
retail_take: Routine regulatory disclosure of voting rights denominator; shareholders use this figure to determine if they must notify changes in holdings. Non-event for market.

headline: New Corporate Presentation
brief: Richmond Hill Resources publishes updated investor presentation on Martello Gold Project in Ontario, Canada.
summary: Richmond Hill Resources plc has released an updated corporate presentation covering its Martello Gold Project located in Ontario, Canada. The presentation includes details on the project's geological framework, priority exploration targets including Sakoose and Maw, and outlines planned next phases of work. The document is available on the company's website.
retail_take: Routine investor communication updating stakeholders on exploration progress; suggests active project development but lacks material operational or financial updates to drive near-term market movement.
